old_exact: $Hoffarth\ v.\ Hoffarth\ , 2020\ ND\ 218,\ \ 5,949\ N.W.2d\ 824\ (quoting\ Kautzman\ v.\ Doll,\ 2018\ ND\ 23,\ \ 6,905\ N.W.2d\ 744).$
new_exact: Hoffarth v. Hoffarth, 2020 ND 218, ¶ 5, 949 N.W.2d 824 (quoting Kautzman v. Doll, 2018 ND 23, ¶ 6, 905 N.W.2d 744).
evidence: The PDF source shows proper citation formatting with paragraph symbols (¶) and correct spacing between numbers. The DB contains escaped LaTeX-style markup with backslashes ($...$) and malformed numbers (5,949 instead of ¶ 5, 949; 6,905 instead of ¶ 6, 905). This is a clear ingestion/processing defect where the OCR or conversion pipeline failed to properly handle the citation formatting, leaving behind escape sequences and concatenating the paragraph number with the reporter number via an erroneous comma.
note: Located in [¶4], the opening citation sentence. This is the only escaped markup block in the opinion and the first occurrence of this citation pattern in the text.

evidence: PDF pdftotext extract shows 'Anderson v. Anderson, 368 N.W.2d 566, 569 (N.D. 1985).' at line 190. DB contains 39 repetitions of malformed '$Anderson\ v$ .' pattern (623 characters) instead. This is a catastrophic OCR artifact with no possible semantic interpretation—the repeating pattern cannot be intentional court text.
note: CRITICAL DEFECT: 623-character block of garbage that should be a single citation. Appears to be OCR markup corruption where citation was scanned multiple times or OCR engine failed to resolve a scanning artifact.

evidence: Standard ND Supreme Court citation format is '2023 ND 154' (the reporter abbreviation is 'ND'). PDF line 12 shows '2023 ND 154'. DB shows corrupted 'IN 1)' where 'ND' should be, consistent with OCR misreading 'ND' as 'IN' and '.' as '1)'.
note: Occurs in caption line of opinion. Digit/letter confusion in OCR (N→I, D→1), period→parenthesis.

evidence: DB lines 3-4 show '2020 * T n -' and '2023 IN 1) 154' as separate lines. PDF shows only '2023 ND 154' on line 12. The '2020 * T n -' line does not exist in the source and appears to be spurious OCR output (possibly attempting to read '2023 ND' with scanning artifacts).
note: Noise artifact: this line has no counterpart in the PDF and is nonsensical ('2020 * T n -'). Should be deleted entirely. Likely OCR attempt to read a smudge or fold mark.

In the authoritative PDF (2023ND110.pdf page 2), the first citation flows continuously: 'See Interest of A.C., 2022 ND 123, ¶ 5, 975 N.W.2d 567 (the clearly erroneous...'. 2022 ND 123, ¶ 5
,
975 N.W.2d 567
 (the clearly...'. This is an ingestion/OCR artifact where line breaks in the PDF layout were incorrectly preserved as content breaks.
note: Citation is rendered as a continuous sentence in the printed opinion. Collapsing the line breaks restores proper formatting.
